title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: R dynamically typed, function can handed wrong type hand back wrong shape complaint. roxyassert reads structured type annotations roxygen2 docs , document() time, generates per-function argument return assertion helpers — calls assert package — function’s documented contract runtime validation come one source. See README complete annotation grammar; vignette short tour plus pattern use .

title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: Register roclet DESCRIPTION:

title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: type annotation parenthesised token start @param @return description. bare type vector; scalar declared explicitly. devtools::document() (re)writes R/contracts-generated.R assert_args_submit_order() assert_return_submit_order(), committed alongside NAMESPACE.
code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param side (scalar<character in c("BUY", "SELL")>) order side. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric in ]0, Inf[>) order size (positive). #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - order_id (character) exchange order id. #' - quantity (numeric) accepted size. #' - datetime (POSIXct) acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                      # generated }

what: The pattern: an abstract class that enforces its own contract
title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: common use abstract base class whose public methods define uniform interface. Document method’s inputs outputs , let roxyassert generate checks — every concrete subclass inherits contract validated runtime, way way . method names scoped class (assert_args_AbstractStore__get), two classes can share method name without colliding. subclass overrides get() re-uses documented contract simply calling generated helpers.
code: #' @title AbstractStore #' @description A key-value store contract. Subclasses implement `.impl_*`; #'   the public methods validate inputs and returns from the documented types. AbstractStore <- R6::R6Class(   "AbstractStore",   public = list(     #' @description Fetch records by key.     #' @param keys (character) one or more keys to fetch.     #' @param limit (scalar<integer>?) optional max rows.     #' @return (data.table) the matched records:     #' - key (character) the record key.     #' - value (numeric) the stored value.     #' - updated_at (POSIXct) last-write time.     get = function(keys, limit = NULL) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__get(keys, limit)       # generated       result <- private$.impl_get(keys, limit)       return(assert_return_AbstractStore__get(result))  # generated     },      #' @description Write one record.     #' @param key (scalar<character>) the record key.     #' @param value (scalar<numeric>) the value to store.     #' @return (R6<AbstractStore>) self, invisibly (for chaining).     put = function(key, value) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__put(key, value)        # generated       private$.impl_put(key, value)       return(invisible(assert_return_AbstractStore__put(self)))     }   ),   private = list(     .impl_get = function(keys, limit) stop("not implemented"),     .impl_put = function(key, value) stop("not implemented")   ) )

title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: helpers generated whether call . Adopt one method, , ; method typed tags left untouched. never hand-write validation function — even methods choose call one.

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: One home per concept. element type element-level modifiers (, | NA) vector’s length live <> (attach bare atomic type); whole-argument modifiers (?/| NULL, type unions) sit outside. “Bare” means different things category. bare atomic type vector length (scalar<...> way say length 1); bare wildcard () object; bare reference type (function/R6<Class>) single length-1 object; bare composite unconstrained list/table (§2, §6). NA NULL reserved words, never type names — keeps | unambiguous. Element-level binds tighter slot-level. / | NA bind nearest atom left, slot-level | union ?. language recursive. composite contains full annotations bullets, depth. Values copied verbatim; generator never coerces. Every value user writes — interval bound, set element — R expression emitted generated check exactly written (0, .Date("2024-01-01"), c("BUY", "SELL"), ORDER_SIDE). roxyassert performs coercion invents time zone format. Intervals ISO/Bourbaki bracket notation around two expressions. Element/atom type agreement checked inline literals taken trust opaque expressions (S2). type may carry category supports (§2). grammar makes category-level nonsense unrepresentable — interval complex, set logical/raw, fractional expression bound integer (integer bounds literal whole numbers ±Inf), vector function, | NA raw, constraint . Inf/-Inf side-specific sentinels (-Inf low , Inf high ). numeric/Date/POSIXct bound, contrast, opaque expression — 0 .Date(...) parse — whether atom’s class static check (S2), grammatical one.

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: Every base type sits exactly one category, category alone (per-type exceptions) decides modifiers legal: ¹ Sets compare ==/%% apply normalisation. integer whole-day Date sets (.Date/Sys.Date) exact fine; numeric sets floating-point-fragile, POSIXct (sub-day Date) sets precision/time-zone-fragile, discouraged — prefer intervals. Interval bounds set elements Date/POSIXct written ordinary R expressions matching class — .Date("2024-01-01"), .POSIXct("2024-01-01 09:00", tz = "America/New_York") — copied verbatim, user (roxyassert) chooses constructor, format, time zone (S2). Date/POSIXct bound’s class time zone taken trust compared </> (coerce), class/tz mismatch yields silently-wrong range check — keep bound value class/tz. integer bounds literal whole numbers; Inf/-Inf open-end sentinels (S2). ² character factor set constrains realised values (.character(x) %% set); | NA generated check (.character(x) %% set | .na(x)). assert factor’s declared levels() — , use prose (scope, §12). asserts nothing type — generator emits type check, requested length/nullability. explicit escape hatch polymorphic argument, unconstrained list/record field, data.table list-column arbitrary cells (S3). complex/logical/raw take set (degenerate floating-point-fragile), raw NA representation. function/R6 bare length-1 references. composite refined nested bullets (named record / typed columns) — list — parameterised list<T>, homogeneous list whose every element type T. enforced grammar (§3); residual element-type rules static (§4).

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: Tokenizing rules (grammar deterministic practice): One scan order. rexpr region (call_set, Date/POSIXct bound) consumed first, string-aware, bracket-balanced R scan skips string/character literals balances ( [ {. bound/set fully consumed next structural token count. Consequently , | > < [ ] inside balanced rexpr invisible annotation tokenizer; top-level structural tokens seen. Interval scan (single pass). [ ] immediately opens interval. bound scanned string-aware bracket-balanced rexpr scan (rule 1), two bounds separated exactly one top-level comma; interval closed first top-level ] [ appears high bound fully balanced. bound may end ] (e.g. df[["t"]]): balancer consumes first, bracket closes interval. Commas/brackets inside balanced rexpr bound invisible; open interval ]0, Inf[ never unbalances anything. Bound classification (ordered choice). bound matched ordered choice: -Inf/Inf sentinel first, signed_number (maximal munch -?digit+(.digit+)? nothing trailing comma/close), rexpr. bound lexes fully sentinel signed_number literal (subject S2/S4); anything else opaque rexpr, trusted (S2). Set dispatch . next char [ ], parse interval. Otherwise scan R expression: lone maximal-munch ident nothing trailing | , ) > ? name_set; anything containing (/[/operator call_set (bracket-balanced, string-aware). bare name_set may compound indexed — FOO | NA set FOO plus atom-level | NA, foo[1:2] must written call_set . terminating | resolved §5 (NA → atom, NULL/type → slot). vector length comma. Inside vector<...> element atom — (...) | NA — consumed first; atom/length comma first top-level comma remains. scalar<...> length production, top-level comma inside scalar<...> parse error (comma inside interval set fine) — single rule scalar<T, n> rejected. > closes innermost open generic (depth-aware); vector<...> R6<...> close one level. R6 must followed <ident>; bare R6 parse error. .. exactly two dots (maximal munch), valid length; run three dots (...) lexical error. lone . decimal point, valid inside numeric bound; .. bound position lone . length position lexical errors. Everything ) free-text description. roxyassert parses (slot) token; rest line description ignores, : adjacent ((type): desc, (type) desc:) cosmetic — canonical style omits . name bullet single token first ( bullet’s top level; slot balanced-parenthesis group opened (, found string-aware scan annotation. Names contain spaces. Child bullets begin subsequent line strictly greater indentation whose first non-space characters -; - inside description text (line) literal. Reference wildcard shapes — function/R6<Class> bare length-1 references (never scalar<>/vector<>, never /| NA/length); takes /set/| NA accept scalar<>/vector<> length check. Union | NA (one-token look-ahead past |). union member parsed full atom, eagerly consumes optional | NA slot’s union loop resumes; disambiguation token | (NA → element, reserved; NULL/typename → slot). (numeric | character | NA) | NA belongs character. | NULL positional: grammar fixes slot tail, mid-union (numeric | NULL | character) parse error.
code: annotation     ::= "(" slot ")" description? bullets?                    (* everything after ) is free-text description (roxyassert                       ignores it); any ":" adjacent to it is cosmetic.                       bullets gated by S1 *)  slot           ::= type ( "|" type )*  ( ( "|" "NULL" ) | "?" )?                    (* a union of >=1 types; the whole argument may be NULL,                       written EITHER as a "| NULL" alternative OR a trailing "?",                       never both *)  type           ::= atomic | wildcard | reference | composite  atomic         ::= atom                  | "scalar" "<" atom ">"                  | "vector" "<" atom "," length ">"                    (* a bare atom is a vector of any length; vector<> requires a                       length (a length-less vector<atom> is a parse error — use                       bare `atom`) *)  atom           ::= "integer"     ( "in" ( int_interval  | set ) )?  ( "|" "NA" )?                  | "numeric"     ( "in" ( num_interval  | set ) )?  ( "|" "NA" )?                  | temporal      ( "in" ( time_interval | set ) )?  ( "|" "NA" )?                  | enumerable    ( "in" set )?                      ( "|" "NA" )?                  | plain                                            ( "|" "NA" )?                  | "raw"  temporal       ::= "Date" | "POSIXct" enumerable     ::= "character" | "factor" plain          ::= "complex" | "logical"  wildcard       ::= "any"                  | "scalar" "<" "any" ">"                  | "vector" "<" "any" "," length ">"                    (* no in / set / | NA: any asserts nothing about type *)  reference      ::= "function" | "R6" "<" ident ">" composite      ::= "list" ( "<" type ">" )? | "data.table" | "data.frame"                    (* bare `list`/table = unconstrained, or a named record /                       typed columns when refined by nested bullets (S1/S3).                       `list<T>` is a HOMOGENEOUS list: EVERY element satisfies the                       type T — list<character>, list<scalar<numeric>>,                       list<R6<Engine>>, list<any>, list<data.table>. T is a `type`                       (no slot-level union / `| NULL` / `?`); a `list<T>` is a leaf                       and takes no bullets (S1, S3). *)  int_interval   ::= low int_lo  "," int_hi  high num_interval   ::= low num_lo  "," num_hi  high time_interval  ::= low time_lo "," time_hi high low            ::= "["    (* closed *) | "]"   (* open *) high           ::= "]"    (* closed *) | "["   (* open *) int_lo         ::= "-"? digit+ | "-Inf" int_hi         ::= "-"? digit+ | "Inf" num_lo         ::= signed_number | "-Inf" | rexpr num_hi         ::= signed_number | "Inf"  | rexpr time_lo        ::= "-Inf" | rexpr time_hi        ::= "Inf"  | rexpr                    (* Only the LOW bound may be -Inf and only the HIGH may be Inf;                       a wrong-side sentinel ([Inf, 0]) is a parse error. A                       Date/POSIXct bound is an R expression of the matching class,                       emitted verbatim — as.Date("2024-01-01"),                       as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 09:00", tz = "America/New_York"),                       lubridate::ymd_hms("...") — so the user owns the constructor                       and time zone; roxyassert never coerces. *)  set            ::= name_set | call_set name_set       ::= ident       (* a bare constant; a single maximal-munch token,                                   terminated by | , ) > or ?; may NOT be a                                   compound or indexing R expression *) call_set       ::= rexpr       (* a call / index / any expression with brackets                                   or an operator — c("a", "b"), pkg::CONST *)  length         ::= int          (* exactly n *)                  | int ".." int (* inclusive range *)                  | int ".."     (* at least n *)  bullets        ::= bullet+      (* siblings at one indentation depth *) bullet         ::= "- " name " (" slot ")" description? bullets?                    (* everything after ) is free-text description; any ":" is                       cosmetic. trailing bullets gated by S1. *) name           ::= ident | "**" ident "**"   (* bold optional, no semantic effect *)  (* lexical terminals *) int            ::= digit+ signed_number  ::= "-"? digit+ ( "." digit+ )? ident          ::= letter ( letter | digit | "." | "_" )* rexpr          ::= (* any valid R expression; opaque, emitted verbatim *) description    ::= (* free text; the remainder of the same physical line *)

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: context-sensitive constraints checked generator; specified rules, stylistic suggestions. S1 — composite nesting. Nested bullets may follow annotation bullet slot exactly one non-NULL alternative alternative bare list/data.table/data.frame (<T> parameter). slot — including list<T>, leaf — takes children; (data.table | data.frame) bullets rejected (field-set describe?). S2 — bound/set element type lowering. Every interval bound set element R expression emitted verbatim — roxyassert never coerces. inline literal generator checks matches atom’s type: number numeric; whole number written L suffix integer (bare c(1, 2, 3) integer atom rejected — coercion); character literal character factor sets (factor set given character labels, matched via .character(x), footnote 2). Date/POSIXct “type” means S3 class (inherits(x, "Date")), typeof; bound class-matching expression user supplies (.Date(...), .POSIXct(..., tz = ...), lubridate::ymd_hms(...)), user owns constructor/format/tz, class/tz mismatch compares silently wrong. Inf/-Inf open-end sentinels — -Inf low bound, Inf high — denoting “bound side” (comparison omitted), values. | NA interval lowered NA-aware — ((x range) | .na(x)), equivalently checked x[!.na(x)] — parallel set form footnote 2; default (| NA) rejects NA. opaque name_set rexpr taken trust — prefer inline literal matters. S3 — named composite fields, homogeneous lists, list-columns. bulleted list/data.table/data.frame asserts presence named fields/columns listed ((<names> %% names(x)), plus per-column type tables). list<T> instead asserts every element satisfies T, applied element-wise (assert_list_of flat element type; element-wise loop T’s full check T carries length/range/| NA). list<> imposes per-element check — lowers bare assert_list(x), list list<> runtime check. column declared atomic type rejects list-cell — intended way catch accidental list-column; declare one deliberately, type list<T> (cell T, e.g. list<character>) list<>. Positional/unnamed records scope (§12). Field checks apply value non-NULL: | NULL/? value short-circuits . S4 — non-empty interval. literal numeric/integer bounds, low bound must ≤ high bound (strictly < either end open), empty reversed interval (]1, 1[, [5, 1]) — whose check can never pass — rejected generation time. (wrong-side sentinel [Inf, 0] caught earlier, grammar.) Date/POSIXct/rexpr bounds opaque range-checked.

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: | resolved one-token look-ahead past — token follows: NA/NULL reserved can never start type, peek unambiguous. | NA positional (attaches atom left); | NULL fixed grammar slot tail never appears mid-union. | NA valid atom (never closed scalar<>/vector<>, never raw, , reference, composite), binds immediately preceding atom — nearest type left, one per atom. Thus: (scalar<numeric> | NA) invalid; write (scalar<numeric | NA>). (numeric | character | NA) | NA binds character — numeric vector, character vector whose elements may NA. (numeric [0, 1] | NA) | NA attach numeric.

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: list/data.table/data.frame nested bullets fixed named-field structure; without bullets unconstrained list/table. list<T> third form — homogeneous list (list-column) whose every element T (e.g. list<character>, list<R6<Engine>>, list<>).

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
code: # --- bare atomic = vector (any length) --- (character)                       # character vector, length >= 1 (numeric)                         # double vector (integer)                         # integer vector (logical)                         # logical vector (complex)                         # complex vector (raw)                             # raw vector (Date)                            # Date vector (POSIXct)                         # date-time vector (factor)                          # factor of any length  # --- wildcard: any asserts nothing about type --- (any)                             # any R object, unchecked (any?)                            # anything, or NULL (scalar<any>)                     # a single object of any type (vector<any, 3>)                  # length 3, elements of any type(s) (vector<any, 1..>)                # at least one element, any type(s)  # --- scalar (length 1) --- (scalar<character>) (scalar<numeric>) (scalar<complex>) (scalar<raw>)                     # a single byte (scalar<logical | NA>)            # tri-state flag: TRUE / FALSE / NA  # --- reference types: bare, length-1 by nature --- (function)                        # a single function/closure (function?)                       # a function, or NULL (R6<Engine>)                      # a single R6 instance inheriting Engine (R6<Engine> | NULL)               # an Engine, or NULL  # --- homogeneous lists: list<T> = every element is T --- (list<character>)                 # a list, every element a character vector (list<scalar<numeric>>)           # a list of single numbers (list<R6<Engine>>)                # a list of Engine instances (list<function>)                  # a list of callbacks (list<any>)                       # a list of anything (= bare list at runtime) (list<data.table>)                # a list of data.tables  # --- vector with explicit length (every atom type) --- (vector<numeric, 10>)             # exactly 10 (vector<numeric, 1..10>)          # 1 to 10 inclusive (vector<integer, 2..>)            # at least 2 (vector<character, 0..>)          # any length, including 0 (vector<logical, 3>)              # three flags (vector<raw, 32>)                 # a 32-byte hash (vector<factor in c("a", "b"), 2..>)               # set + open length (vector<numeric in [0, 1], 1..>)                   # interval + open-ended length (vector<Date in [as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2024-12-31")], 1..7>)  # rexpr-comma vs length-comma  # --- intervals (ordered atomics only; -Inf is the low sentinel, Inf the high) --- (scalar<numeric in [0, 1]>)       # 0 <= x <= 1 (scalar<numeric in ]0, 1[>)       # 0 <  x <  1 (scalar<numeric in ]0, 1]>)       # 0 <  x <= 1 (scalar<numeric in [-1.5, 2.5]>)  # fractional, signed bounds (scalar<numeric in ]0, Inf[>)     # x > 0 (scalar<numeric in ]-Inf, 0]>)    # x <= 0 (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>)     # x >= 1  (integer bounds are whole numbers) (scalar<integer in ]-Inf, 0]>)    # x <= 0  (-Inf is the low sentinel) (numeric in [0, 1])               # every element in [0, 1] (scalar<Date in [as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2026-12-31")]>)        # bounds are R exprs, verbatim (scalar<Date in [as.Date("2024-01-01"), Inf[>)                          # on or after a date (Inf high sentinel) (scalar<Date in ]-Inf, as.Date("2024-12-31")]>)                         # on or before a date (-Inf low sentinel) (scalar<POSIXct in [as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 00:00", tz = "America/New_York"), Inf[>)  # user owns the tz (scalar<POSIXct in [lubridate::ymd_hms("2024-01-01 00:00:00"), lubridate::ymd_hms("2025-01-01 00:00:00")[>)  # any constructor  # --- sets / enums (ordered + enumerable atomics) --- (scalar<character in c("BUY", "SELL")>)   # inline set, scalar (character in c("BUY", "SELL"))           # vector, every element in the set (scalar<character in ORDER_SIDE>)         # set from a bare constant name (integer in c(1L, 2L, 3L))                # exact integer enum (bare vector) (scalar<integer in c(1L, 2L, 3L)>)        # exact integer enum (scalar) (factor in c("low", "med", "high"))       # constrains realised values (footnote 2) (Date in c(as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2024-06-30")))   # set elements are R exprs, verbatim (numeric in c(0.25, 0.5, 1.0))            # discouraged: floating-point ==  # --- NA permission (atomics except raw; default: NA not allowed) --- (numeric | NA)                    # numeric vector, NAs allowed (scalar<numeric | NA>)            # one numeric or NA (scalar<integer | NA>)            # one integer or NA (scalar<POSIXct | NA>)            # one timestamp or NA (complex | NA)                    # complex vector, NAs allowed (vector<numeric | NA, 10>)        # 10 numerics, NAs allowed (numeric in [0, 1] | NA)          # constrained + NA-allowed (factor in c("low", "med", "high") | NA)   # missing-category factor  # --- nullable slot (whole argument) --- (scalar<numeric>?)                # one numeric, or NULL (scalar<numeric> | NULL)          # identical to the above (use one, not both) (character?)                      # character vector, or NULL  # --- type unions (slot level) --- (numeric | character)             # a numeric vector OR a character vector (numeric | character | NA)        # numeric, OR character with NAs allowed (R6<Reader> | R6<Writer>)         # either R6 class (data.table | NULL)               # a data.table or NULL  # --- everything at once --- (vector<numeric in ]0, 1] | NA, 1..100>?) # NULL, OR a numeric vector of length 1..100 whose elements lie in (0,1] and may be NA

what: 8. Demo 1 — every inline form on one function
title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
code: #' Place a batch of orders. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) the `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param sides (character in c("BUY", "SELL")) one side per order. #' @param quantities (vector<numeric in ]0, Inf[, 1..500>) positive sizes, up to 500. #' @param limits (vector<numeric in ]0, Inf[ | NA, 1..500>) limit prices; NA = market. #' @param leverage (scalar<integer in [1, 125]>?) leverage, or NULL for spot. #' @param tags (vector<character, 0..>) free-form labels (possibly none). #' @param tag (scalar<character> | NULL) optional client tag. #' @param venue (scalar<character in VENUES>) a known venue id. #' @param tier (scalar<factor in c("retail", "vip")>) account tier. #' @param dry_run (scalar<logical | NA>) simulate only; NA = use account default. #' @param not_before (scalar<POSIXct>?) earliest send time, or NULL. #' @param on_fill (function?) optional fill callback. #' @param userdata (any?) opaque payload passed back to `on_fill`, or NULL. #' @param config (list) execution config (a bulleted composite param, S1): #' - slippage_bps (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>) allowed slippage. #' - retries (scalar<integer in [0, 5]>) max retries. #' @return (data.table) the acknowledgements (see Demo 2 for nested returns). #' @export place_batch <- function(symbol, sides, quantities, limits, leverage = NULL,                         tags = character(), tag = NULL, venue, tier, dry_run,                         not_before = NULL, on_fill = NULL, userdata = NULL,                         config = list()) {   assert_args_place_batch(symbol, sides, quantities, limits, leverage,                           tags, tag, venue, tier, dry_run, not_before, on_fill,                           userdata, config)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_place_batch(result)) }

what: 9. Demo 2 — the kitchen sink: a deeply nested composite return
title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
code: #' Run a full report. #' #' @param symbols (character) one or more `BASE/QUOTE` pairs. #' @param top_n (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) rows to keep per section. #' @return (list) the report: #' - **status** (scalar<character in c("ok", "partial", "failed")>) overall outcome. #' - generated_at (scalar<POSIXct>) when the report was produced. #' - window (scalar<Date in [as.Date("2000-01-01"), as.Date("2100-01-01")]>) as-of date. #' - sections (list) one entry per requested view: #'   - matches (data.table) ranked matches: #'     - symbol (character) the pair. #'     - score (numeric in [0, 1]) normalised rank score. #'     - drawdown (numeric in ]-Inf, 0]) worst observed drawdown. #'     - side (factor in c("BUY", "SELL")) order side. #'     - flags (character | NA) label(s), NA where none apply. #'     - extras (list<numeric>) a list-column; each cell a numeric vector. #'   - rejected (data.table | NULL) rows dropped, or NULL if none: #'     - symbol (character) the pair. #'     - reason (scalar<character in c("liquidity", "filter", "error")>) why. #'   - cursor (scalar<character>?) next-page cursor, or NULL at the end. #' - audit (data.frame) a flat audit log: #'   - at (POSIXct) event time. #'   - level (factor in c("info", "warn", "error")) severity. #'   - message (character) the message. #'   - context (list<any>) an arbitrary list-column (no per-cell check). #' - diagnostics (list) run diagnostics: #'   - warnings (vector<character, 0..>) messages (possibly none). #'   - retries (scalar<integer in [0, Inf[>) retry count. #'   - timings (list) millisecond timings: #'     - parse_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>) parse time. #'     - run_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>) run time. #'     - per_source (data.table) a row per source: #'       - source (character) source id. #'       - ms (numeric in [0, Inf[) time for that source. #' @export report <- function(symbols, top_n) {   assert_args_report(symbols, top_n)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_report(result)) }

what: 10. Demo 3 — an abstract R6 class enforcing every return kind
title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
code: #' @title AbstractStore #' @description A store contract; subclasses implement `.impl_*`. Each public #'   method validates its inputs and its return from the documented types. AbstractStore <- R6::R6Class(   "AbstractStore",   public = list(     #' @description Fetch records by key.     #' @param keys (character) keys to fetch.     #' @param limit (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>?) optional max rows.     #' @return (data.table) the records:     #' - key (character) the key.     #' - value (numeric | NA) the value, NA if unset.     #' - updated_at (POSIXct) last write time.     get = function(keys, limit = NULL) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__get(keys, limit)       return(assert_return_AbstractStore__get(private$.impl_get(keys, limit)))     },      #' @description Count records.     #' @return (scalar<integer in [0, Inf[>) the count.     count = function() {       return(assert_return_AbstractStore__count(private$.impl_count()))     },      #' @description Write one record; returns self for chaining.     #' @param key (scalar<character>) the key.     #' @param value (scalar<numeric> | NULL) the value, or NULL to clear it.     #' @return (R6<AbstractStore>) self.     put = function(key, value) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__put(key, value)       private$.impl_put(key, value)       return(invisible(assert_return_AbstractStore__put(self)))     },      #' @description Fetch one record, or NULL if absent.     #' @param key (scalar<character>) the key.     #' @return (list | NULL) the record, or NULL:     #' - key (scalar<character>) the key.     #' - value (scalar<numeric | NA>) the value.     find = function(key) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__find(key)       return(assert_return_AbstractStore__find(private$.impl_find(key)))     }   ),   private = list(     .impl_get = function(keys, limit) stop("not implemented"),     .impl_count = function() stop("not implemented"),     .impl_put = function(key, value) stop("not implemented"),     .impl_find = function(key) stop("not implemented")   ) )

what: 11. Demo 4 — corner cases and tricky combinations
title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
code: # union mixing a constrained vector, NA permission, and NULL (numeric in [0, 1] | NA | character)? # read: NULL, OR (a numeric vector in [0,1], NAs allowed) OR (a character vector)  # scalar union, nullable (scalar<integer in [1, 6]> | scalar<character in c("d6")>)?  # length-pinned, NA-allowed, range-constrained, nullable (vector<numeric in [-1, 1] | NA, 3>?)  # set from a bare constant, as a vector (character in CURRENCIES)  # reference / wildcard, nullable / unioned (function?)                       # a function, or NULL (R6<Engine> | NULL)               # an Engine, or NULL (R6<Reader> | R6<Writer>)         # either class (any)                             # truly polymorphic argument, unchecked  # a data.table with list-columns (type the column list<T>) (data.table) # - id (character) identifier. # - tags (list<character>) a list-column; each cell a character vector. # - blob (list<any>) a list-column of arbitrary cells (no per-cell check).  # nested list whose only field is a nullable nested table (list) # - page (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) page number. # - rows (data.table | NULL) the page, or NULL when empty: #   - id (character) identifier. #   - amount (numeric in ]0, Inf[ | NA) positive amount, NA if pending.  # --- intentionally INVALID --- # (scalar<numeric, 1>)            # scalar takes no length # (scalar<numeric> | NA)          # | NA must be inside <>: use (scalar<numeric | NA>) # (numeric | NULL?)               # pick one nullability marker, not both # (complex in [0, 1])             # interval on a non-ordered type # (character in [0, 1])           # interval on a non-ordered type (use a set) # (integer in [0.5, 2.5])         # fractional bounds on integer # (numeric in [Inf, 0])           # Inf is the HIGH sentinel only; -Inf the low # (numeric in ]1, 1[)             # S4: empty / reversed interval (never satisfiable) # (logical in c(TRUE, FALSE))     # logical takes no set (degenerate) # (complex in c(0+0i, 1+0i))      # complex takes no set # (raw in OPCODES)                # raw takes no set # (raw | NA)                      # raw has no NA representation # (any in c(1, 2))                # any takes no constraint # (any | NA)                      # any takes no | NA # (scalar<function>)              # function is bare: write (function) # (vector<function, 3>)           # function is a length-1 reference (scope choice, §12) # (R6 | NULL)                     # R6 must name a class: R6<Class> # (data.table | NA)               # | NA is element-level; not valid on a composite # (data.table | data.frame): ...  # S1: bullets need a single bare composite # (vector<numeric>)               # vector<> requires a length — use bare (numeric) # (list<numeric>): ...            # S1: list<T> is a leaf and takes no bullets

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: scope current grammar — express prose hand-written check. (Homogeneous/element-typed collections, lists callbacks, columns model objects longer — now list<T>, e.g. list<scalar<numeric>>, list<function>, list<R6<Model>>.) Composite cardinality — “data.table 1..N rows”, “list<T> exactly 3 elements”. Length applies atomic/vector<>; list<T> unbounded length. vector reference types — vector<function> / vector<R6<...>> rejected (references length-1). collection fine expressible: list<function>, list<R6<Engine>>. Positional / unnamed records — list bullets describe named fields; positional (unnamed) record scope. (homogeneous unnamed list list<T>.) factor’s declared levels() — set checks realised values, level schema (footnote 2).

title: The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference
text: Every base type sits exactly one category (§2); every modifier carries ✅ category’s row — per-type exceptions. [interval] appears integer/numeric/Date/POSIXct; integer bounds literal whole numbers / ±Inf (expression); numeric/Date/POSIXct bounds expressions whose class S2 checks (Date [0, 1] parses S2 rejects ); -Inf low bound, Inf high. c(set) appears ordered + enumerable atomics; never complex/logical/raw/; exact integer/whole-day Date, discouraged numeric/POSIXct (==); factor/character constrains realised values, levels, NA-aware | NA (footnote 2). interval/set bound types match atom, intervals non-empty (S2, S4). | NA appears atom (bare inside <>), never raw, , reference, composite; binds nearest atom left; never closed scalar<>/vector<>. ? | NULL mean thing never appear together. | followed NA (element), NULL (slot), type (union); union member carries | NA. carries /set/| NA emits type check; length (scalar<>/vector<, n>) nullability apply. intervals use square brackets ([/] low, ]/[ high — closed/open, combination); never ( )/{ }; one top-level comma; brackets/commas inside rexpr bound invisible tokenizer. sets bare name_set (single token) call_set (call/index/operator expression); never { }; enum construct (word appears prose). vector<> requires length (, n / ..b / ..); length-less vector<T> rejected (use bare T); length appears inside vector<>; scalar<T, n> rejected; .. one token, valid length position. function/R6<Class> appear bare reference types; R6 always names class. list/data.table/data.frame carry /| NA/length/vector<>; refined nested bullets (named record / typed columns, S1/S3) , list, parameterised list<T> T single type (homogeneous, slot-union/| NULL/?, leaf bullets); list<> ≡ bare list runtime; list-column typed list<T>, atomic-typed column rejects list-cells. everything ) free-text description (roxyassert ignores ); : adjacent cosmetic; canonical style omits . generated names assert_args_<fn> / assert_return_<fn>, assert_args_<Class>__<method> / assert_return_<Class>__<method> R6 (double underscore). ```

what: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Write contract — roxygen2 docs — generate runtime checks. roxyassert turns structured type annotations roxygen2 documentation per-function assertion helpers. document() time parses @param/@return tags generates argument return checks — plain calls assert package — committed source file can read debug. R dynamically typed: nothing stops caller passing character expected number, function returning wrong shape. usual fix hand-write validation function — repeats, code, types already wrote docs, drifts . roxyassert makes documentation single source truth: annotations render humans generate checks, disagree.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: One source truth. typed @param/@return write humans generates runtime checks — can never drift. magic, coercion. Value constraints (ranges, sets) plain R expressions copied verbatim generated check. write .POSIXct("2024-01-01", tz = "America/New_York") roxyassert pastes — never guesses format, constructor, time zone behalf. open value universe. Interval bounds set elements arbitrary R, can range- membership-check value type — Date, POSIXct, lubridate, bit64, classes — without roxyassert “know ” . (set declared types fixed; wildcard value deliberately don’t constrain.) Readable, debuggable output. generated helpers ordinary assert calls committed file can open, read, step .

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: roxyassert roxygen2 roclet — runs documentation step (one writes NAMESPACE man/*.Rd), never R CMD build/check. parses tag text ; never executes code. assert depend roxyassert stays lightweight; roxyassert depends assert + roxygen2.
code: R/*.R  (your code + typed roxygen tags)      |      |   devtools::document()   <- roxyassert runs here, as a roclet      v   R/contracts-generated.R       <- generated assert_args_* / assert_return_* helpers      |      |   you call them inside your functions, and commit the file      v   runtime validation via the `assert` package

text: Register roclet package’s DESCRIPTION: devtools::document() runs roxyassert alongside built-roclets (re)writes R/contracts-generated.R.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: type annotation parenthesised token start @param @return description. Tags without leading (...) token ignored, adoption incremental opt-. section working overview; complete grammar reference (formal EBNF, type categories, precedence, exhaustive demos) authoritative specification. Default arity follows R: bare atomic type vector length. scalar (length 1) declared explicitly scalar<...>. (Reference types — function/R6 — length-1, bare composite unconstrained list/table.) <> generic wraps element type (, vector, length); whole-argument modifiers sit outside : Shape (<>): scalar<...>, vector<..., length>, R6<...>. bare atomic type already vector, reach <> need length 1 (scalar), explicit length, R6 class. Element constraints: <interval--set> | NA attach element type — bare wrapped. (numeric [0, Inf[) non-negative numeric vector; (scalar<numeric [0, Inf[>) single non-negative number. Slot (whole argument): trailing ? (may NULL) | type-unions. bracket / token reference: < > — generics: scalar / vector wrap element type (+ length), R6 names class. [ ] / ] [ — numeric interval ([/] closed, ]/[ open, ISO/Bourbaki). — value-constraint element type: interval, R set. c(...) / name — set allowed values (enum). , — vector length (inside <>). | NA — elements may NA (bare wrapped); default: allowed. ? / | NULL — whole argument may NULL (slot level). | <type> — union another type, e.g. numeric | character. | operator read follows : NA = elements may missing, NULL = whole argument may NULL, anything else = type union.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: type token annotation column/field bullet (subject per-category rules — scalar<>/vector<> wrap atomics ; function/R6/data.table/data.frame written bare; list written bare, refined bullets, parameterised list<T>): function R6<Class> reference types: written bare ((function), (R6<Engine>)), nullable slot ((R6<Engine>?)), never wrapped scalar<>/vector<> never carrying /| NA/length. Intervals ([ , ]) apply ordered types (integer/numeric/Date/POSIXct); sets (c(...)) apply ordered enumerable atomics (integer/numeric/Date/POSIXct/character/factor) — complex, logical, raw, take set. asserts nothing type (length/nullability ). See grammar reference full per-category rules.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: vector length (default) — (character) scalar (length 1) — (scalar<character>) exactly n — (vector<numeric, 10>) length range / least n — (vector<numeric, 1..10>) / (vector<numeric, 2..>) 1 5 (inclusive) — (scalar<numeric [1, 5]>) greater 0 — (scalar<numeric ]0, Inf[>) 1 — (scalar<numeric ]-Inf, 1]>) every element vector range — (numeric [1, 5]) enum, inline set (scalar) — (scalar<character c("BUY", "SELL")>) enum, vector constant — (character ORDER_SIDE) NA elements allowed — (numeric | NA) nullable slot — (scalar<numeric>?) ≡ (scalar<numeric> | NULL) (use one, ) union types — (numeric | character) R6 instance — (R6<Engine>) (type check) — () homogeneous list / list-column — (list<character>) / (list<>) Everything composes. example (vector<numeric ]0, 1] | NA, 10>) means: numeric vector length 10, every element (0, 1], NA allowed.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: list, data.table, data.frame describe contents markdown bullet list beneath tag. Bullets nest arbitrarily, can compose tables inside lists inside lists. bullet - name (type) description; : ) tolerated optional, bold around name optional (- **name** (type) ... also works). column declared atomic type checked type, column declared (numeric) actually holds list fails — intended way catch accidental list-column. declare one purpose, type list<T> (cell T) list<> (arbitrary cells). list<T> also write homogeneous list — list<function> (callbacks), list<R6<Model>> (model objects):
code: #' @return (list) the query result: #' - ok (scalar<logical>) whether the query succeeded. #' - rows (data.table) matched rows: #'   - id (character) row identifier. #'   - value (numeric in [0, Inf[) the (non-negative) value. #' - meta (list) pagination metadata: #'   - page (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) current page. #'   - cursor (scalar<character>?) next-page cursor, or NULL at the end. #' @return (data.table) rows: #' - id (character) identifier. #' - tags (list<character>) a list-column; each cell a character vector. #' - meta (list<any>) a list-column of arbitrary cells (unchecked).

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param side (scalar<character in c("BUY", "SELL")>) order side. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric in ]0, Inf[>) order size (positive). #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric in ]0, Inf[>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - order_id (character) exchange order id. #' - status (character) order status. #' - quantity (numeric) accepted size. #' - datetime (POSIXct) acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                      # generated }

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
code: #' Run a report. #' #' @param symbols (character) one or more `BASE/QUOTE` pairs. #' @param top_n (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) how many rows to keep. #' @return (list) the report: #' - status (scalar<character in c("ok", "partial", "failed")>) outcome. #' - result (list) the payload: #'   - rows (data.table) ranked rows: #'     - symbol (character) the pair. #'     - score (numeric in [0, Inf[) rank score. #'   - pagination (list) cursor state: #'     - page (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) current page. #'     - cursor (scalar<character>?) next cursor, or NULL at the end. #' - diagnostics (list) run diagnostics: #'   - warnings (character) messages (possibly length 0). #'   - timings (list) millisecond timings: #'     - parse_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>) parse time. #'     - run_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>) run time. #' @export report <- function(symbols, top_n) {   assert_args_report(symbols, top_n)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_report(result)) }

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: See Getting started vignette fuller pattern: abstract class whose every method enforces inputs outputs docs.
code: #' Open orders for a symbol. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) the pair. #' @param statuses (character in c("OPEN", "PARTIALLY_FILLED")) statuses to include. #' @return (data.table) the open orders, newest first: #' - order_id (character) exchange id. #' - side (factor) BUY or SELL. #' - quantity (numeric in ]0, Inf[) remaining size. #' - reduce_only (logical) reduce-only flag. #' - created_at (POSIXct) submission time. #' @export open_orders <- function(symbol, statuses) {   assert_args_open_orders(symbol, statuses)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_open_orders(result)) }

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Two helpers, optional. assert_args_<fn> emitted function least one typed @param; assert_return_<fn> @return carries parseable type. Explicit arguments. assert_args_<fn>(arg1, arg2, ...) takes documented parameters name, declaration order, contract visible call site. Returns passed . assert_return_<fn>(value) validates returns value unchanged, can write return(assert_return_fn(x)) keep invisible(). invisible() irrelevant — generation depends typed @return, value returned. R6 methods scoped class. Method submit class Engine generates assert_args_Engine__submit (double-underscore separator), two classes sharing method name never collide; clashes abort generation rather overwrite. Internal, undocumented, committed. Generated helpers exported carry .Rd; live single R/contracts-generated.R (--edit banner) commit like NAMESPACE. package generates uses . Using optional. helpers generated regardless; calling opt-. Adopt one function, , — function typed tags simply left alone.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Packages like typed declare types inside function body custom operator. works checks runtime, changes every function written. roxyassert keeps R code exactly treats documentation already write contract — checks generated , never write validation function hand , even choose call every one.
